name: mypy
what_it_does: Official Python static type checker that checks Python code for type errors using Python's type hint annotations (PEP 484). Mypy catches type mismatches, wrong argument types, missing return types, and attribute errors at analysis time without running the code. The reference implementation of Python's gradual typing system — supported by Python's core team.
use_cases: Add static type checking to Python codebases to catch type errors before runtime without full annotation coverage
Enforce type safety on function signatures and return types in production Python services
Use in CI/CD pipelines as a gating step to prevent type regressions from entering production
Gradually adopt types in existing codebases using --ignore-missing-imports and per-module strictness
Generate type stubs for type-checking third-party libraries that lack type annotations
not_for: Runtime type enforcement — mypy is static analysis only; use Pydantic for runtime validation
JavaScript/TypeScript projects — use tsc (TypeScript compiler) or similar
Replacing tests — mypy catches type errors but not logic errors; tests are still required
best_when: You want to progressively add type safety to Python codebases with the reference Python type checker and maximum ecosystem compatibility.
avoid_when: You want faster type checking — Pyright (from Microsoft) is significantly faster for large codebases and has better VS Code integration.

auth.notes: CLI tool and library with no auth requirement.

pricing.notes: Free and open source, developed by Dropbox and the Python community.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — local tool. Mypy can be slow on large codebases; use --cache-dir and daemon mode (dmypy) for incremental checking.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Mypy errors include file, line, and error code (e.g., [arg-type], [return-value]). Error messages describe expected vs actual types. --show-error-codes enables error code filtering in CI.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Static analysis is deterministic and idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: mypy checks ONLY files with type annotations by default — add # type: ignore or --check-untyped-defs to check unannotated functions; without this, many errors are silently skipped
Third-party libraries without type stubs cause 'Cannot find implementation or library stub' errors — use --ignore-missing-imports or install types-* stub packages (types-requests, types-PyYAML)
Mypy's type narrowing requires explicit isinstance() checks — Union[str, int] narrows to str only inside if isinstance(x, str) block; re-assigning variables breaks narrowing
Optional[X] (same as Union[X, None]) requires explicit None checks before attribute access — mypy flags x.attr as error if x: Optional[X] without narrowing
TypeVar and Generic types have complex interaction with mypy's inference — agents generating generic code should test with strict mode enabled
Mypy daemon (dmypy) is much faster for incremental checks but can get stale — restart with dmypy stop and dmypy run after significant code changes

security_score_components.security_notes: Pure static analysis tool with no network access in normal use. Plugin system could theoretically execute malicious code — audit mypy plugins before enabling.
